answer:
Binance developer API access is free and self-serve for all users [1][2]. There are no subscription fees, per-call charges, or costs to generate an API key [1][2]. You only pay standard trading fees when executing trades via the API, identical to those on the web interface [1][2].

To get started, create an account, complete identity verification, enable two-factor authentication, and make a deposit to your Spot Wallet [3]. You can then generate API keys through the API Management section of your account [3][1]. Public market data is available without authentication, while private operations require signed requests using your API key and secret [1][2].
